model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"With a fixed 256-step budget, doubling batch size exposes the model to substantially more of CIFAR-10 within the same step budget, which is a strong lever for this undertrained small CNN.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 8","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 32","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The model is still extremely small relative to the fixed-step budget. A modest width increase should improve representation capacity without introducing architectural complexity or a dramatic runtime jump.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 2","old":"DEPTH = 1","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","function":"","new":"","old":"","op":"insert_after","source":"","text":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),"}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The current wider model improved substantially, which suggests it is still capacity-limited. Adding one more residual block is a simple way to increase depth and nonlinear refinement without changing the overall training setup.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BASE_CHANNELS = 16","old":"BASE_CHANNELS = 12","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 48","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Depth increases are paying off, so the next low-complexity capacity lever is a moderate width bump. Raising channels and head width should improve feature quality without changing the successful optimizer and schedule setup.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The model and batch size have both grown while training still only lasts 256 steps, so the current LR is likely conservative. A moderate LR increase should improve optimization speed without disturbing the working schedule.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The previous LR increase improved validation loss again, so there is likely still headroom. A smaller follow-on step to 2e-3 keeps the same training recipe while testing whether optimization can move faster within the fixed 256-step horizon.","secondary_modes":["summaries","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"With only 256 optimization steps and no label noise, this model is more likely underfitting than overfitting. Reducing weight decay slightly should preserve the improved optimization dynamics while relaxing unnecessary regularization.","secondary_modes":["topk","layout"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"At 128, the model still sees well under one full pass over the 50k training set within 256 steps. Increasing batch size toward full-dataset coverage is a high-leverage way to use the fixed-step budget more efficiently while preserving the strong optimizer configuration.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The larger batch improved loss substantially, which often leaves room for a corresponding LR increase. Raising LR moderately should help the model capitalize on the more stable large-batch gradients within the same 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The larger batch regime continues to pay off, and 192 still does not quite cover the full 50k-example training set in 256 steps. A smaller follow-up increase should improve data coverage again while being less risky than jumping straight to 256.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 224","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"The last batch-size increase produced another strong loss drop, and 256 cleanly pushes the run past full-dataset coverage within the fixed 256-step budget. This is the simplest continuation of the most reliable trend so far.","secondary_modes":["topk","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 2.5e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The run keeps benefiting from larger effective batch, and the current LR likely remains slightly conservative for batch 256. A modest increase to 3e-3 is the simplest way to test whether optimization can move faster without changing the successful architecture.","secondary_modes":["micro","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"The model is still trained for only 256 steps, and reducing weight decay already helped slightly. Pushing regularization all the way off is a reasonable next test for this short-budget, clean-label setting.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"DEPTH = 3","old":"DEPTH = 2","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""},{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),\n            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),\n            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","old":"            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),            ResidualBlock(channels, dropout=DROPOUT_RATE, use_bn=USE_BATCHNORM),","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"Earlier depth increases gave some of the largest gains in the run, and the current configuration still appears capacity-limited. Adding one more residual block is a focused test of whether extra refinement depth beats the already tuned optimizer and batch regime.","secondary_modes":["micro","topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 3.5e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"The deeper model improved immediately, which suggests the current LR may again be slightly conservative for the new capacity. A moderate increase is the cheapest way to test whether the larger network can optimize faster within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["layout","summaries"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The backbone has grown substantially while the classifier head remains relatively small. Increasing the hidden width is a low-risk capacity increase with minimal runtime impact compared with adding more convolutional depth or width.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"layout","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"FC_HIDDEN = 128","old":"FC_HIDDEN = 96","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"layout","rationale":"The previous head-width increase improved validation loss again, so the classifier is still a live bottleneck. A final bump to 128 is a cheap capacity increase with much lower runtime risk than widening the convolutional trunk further.","secondary_modes":["topk","micro"]}
